name: Pitcairn Island
summary: Pitcairn is a tiny green volcanic island in the South Pacific, one of the most remote inhabited places on Earth. Only about 35 to 50 people live in its single village, Adamstown, and most are descendants of the HMS Bounty mutineers.
funFacts: In 1789 sailor Fletcher Christian led a famous mutiny on HMS Bounty, then hid on Pitcairn and burned the ship so the Royal Navy could not find them.
Pitcairn has only about 35 to 50 people, making it the world's smallest country by population, and many islanders share the surname Christian.
Islanders speak Pitkern, a fun mash-up of old sailor English and Tahitian, with phrases like 'whata way you?' meaning 'how are you?'
Pitcairn sits inside one of the planet's biggest marine reserves, more than 800,000 square kilometres of protected ocean full of sharks and whales.
Big ships cannot dock at Pitcairn, so islanders zoom out in wooden longboats from Bounty Bay and climb up rope ladders to trade with visitors.
thingsToDo: Watch Pitcairners power out from Bounty Bay in their wooden longboat and climb a rope ladder up the side of the visiting ship.
Stand at Bounty Bay, the only landing spot, where Fletcher Christian's mutineers came ashore in January 1790.
Hike up to Christian's Cave, the rocky lookout where Fletcher Christian once watched the sea for any approaching Royal Navy ships.
See the original Bounty Bible kept safely on display inside the little white Adamstown church.
Splash and snorkel in St Paul's Pool, a clear natural rock pool tucked between the cliffs at the edge of the ocean.
Buy a colourful Pitcairn stamp or a jar of island honey from one of the world's smallest post offices.

wildlife.name: Pitcairn Reed Warbler
wildlife.description: Small brown songbird that lives only on Pitcairn and nowhere else in the world.

wildlife.name: Henderson Petrel
wildlife.description: Dark seabird that nests almost entirely on nearby Henderson Island, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

wildlife.name: Green Sea Turtle
wildlife.description: Big gentle turtle that glides through Pitcairn's warm reefs eating sea grass and algae.

wildlife.name: Humpback Whale
wildlife.description: Giant ocean singer that swims past Pitcairn each year on its long Pacific journey.

localWord.meaning: How are you? — a friendly Pitkern greeting used by Pitcairn islanders.

images.alt: The dramatic green cliffs and rocky coastline of Pitcairn Island rising from the deep blue Pacific.
The tiny village of Adamstown, the only settlement on Pitcairn Island.
A ship standing off Bounty Bay, the only landing place on Pitcairn Island.
Christian's Cave high on the cliff, where Fletcher Christian once kept watch over the sea.
St Paul's Pool, a clear natural rock pool ringed by black volcanic cliffs.
The original HMS Bounty Bible, kept on display in Adamstown's little church.
A Pitcairn reed warbler perched on a branch, a little brown songbird found only on Pitcairn.
A Henderson petrel in flight over the Pitcairn Islands.
A green sea turtle gliding up to the surface in the warm Pacific Ocean.
A humpback whale leaping out of the ocean.
